title: Wrong result with enable_analyzer when query uses SELECT * REPLACE + WHERE on same column
description:
### Describe what's wrong
When replacing a column value by a `SELECT * REPLACE`, the WHERE clause still uses the table's value.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE events
(
`app_id` UInt64,
`network` String,
`name_asset_group_id` UInt64,
`date` Date
) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO events VALUES (0,  'snapchat', 123, '2023-08-04'),  (42, 'snapchat', 0,   '2023-08-04');
SELECT 'new analyzer', *
REPLACE (if(app_id = 0, 1, app_id) AS app_id)
FROM   events
WHERE  app_id != 0;
SET enable_analyzer = 0;
SELECT 'old analyzer',*
REPLACE (if(app_id = 0, 1, app_id) AS app_id)
FROM   events
WHERE  app_id != 0;
----
new analyzer	42	snapchat	0	2023-08-04
old analyzer	1	snapchat	123	2023-08-04
old analyzer	42	snapchat	0	2023-08-04
```
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/dcfea6a8-16f8-4c69-9001-9d07263106cf
### Expected behavior
I can use REPLACed value in WHERE
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE events_v2 (app_id BIGINT, network VARCHAR, name_asset_group_id BIGINT, date DATE, status VARCHAR);
SELECT 'new analyzer', *, CASE WHEN app_id = 0 THEN 1 ELSE app_id END AS app_id_replaced FROM events_v2 WHERE app_id != 0 AND status IS NOT NULL;
SELECT 'old analyzer', *, CASE WHEN app_id = 0 THEN 1 ELSE app_id END AS app_id_replaced FROM events_v2 WHERE app_id != 0 AND status IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: SELECT * REPLACE with WHERE on same column
